name: Berlin (Germany)

colors.symbolism: the central field on which the bear stands
the upper and lower stripes, recalling the heraldic colours of Brandenburg
the outline of the Berlin bear

description: A white horizontal band between two narrower red stripes; in the centre toward the hoist, a rampant black bear with red claws and tongue.
history: The Berlin civil flag (Landesflagge) was adopted on 26 May 1954, with the bear redesigned in its current form in 1981. The bear has appeared on Berlin's seal since 1280; the red-white-red striped layout was introduced after the Second World War to distinguish the modern state from earlier Prussian heraldry.

features.role: Berlin bear (rampant black bear with red claws and tongue)
features.symbolism: The bear has been the heraldic symbol of Berlin since the 13th century; popular etymology links it to the city's name (although the name actually derives from a Slavic root).
